title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself poses minimal risk, but improper implementation or over-reliance on automated tools without proper validation could lead to missed vulnerabilities or false positives. Additionally, if the tool is compromised, it could introduce malicious code into the scanning process.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool's source code and security practices before integrating it into workflows.
Use the tool as a supplementary measure alongside manual code reviews and other security practices.
Monitor the tool for updates and community feedback to ensure it remains secure and effective.

title: The Elasticsearch logging provider, when configured with a `host` URL that embeds credentials ...
description: The Elasticsearch logging provider, when configured with a `host` URL that embeds credentials (for example `https://user:password@server.example.com:9200`), wrote the full host URL — including the embedded credentials — into task logs. Any user with task-log read permission could harvest the backend credentials. Users are advised to upgrade to `apache-airflow-providers-elasticsearch` 6.5.3 or later and, as a defense-in-depth measure, configure the backend credentials via a secret backend rather

title:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

rxrpc: Also unshare DATA...
description:
In the Linux kernel, the following vulnerability has been resolved:

rxrpc: Also unshare DATA/RESPONSE packets when paged frags are present

The DATA-packet handler in rxrpc_input_call_event() and the RESPONSE
handler in rxrpc_verify_response() copy the skb to a linear one before
calling into the security ops only when skb_cloned() is true.  An skb
that is not cloned but still carries externally-owned paged fragments
(e.g. SKBFL_SHARED_FRAG set by splice() into a UDP socket via
__ip_append_data,

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in the Linux kernel's rxrpc subsystem could allow attackers to exploit improperly handled paged fragments in network packets, potentially leading to privilege escalation, data leakage, or denial of service. This issue may particularly impact systems relying on UDP-based communication.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the latest security patches to the Linux kernel to address CVE-2026-43500.
Conduct a thorough review of systems using the rxrpc subsystem or UDP-based communication to identify potential exposure.
Implement network monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify any suspicious activity related to this vulnerability.

title:
WebDyne::Session versions through 2.075 for Perl generates the session id insecurely.

The...
description:
WebDyne::Session versions through 2.075 for Perl generates the session id insecurely.

The session handler generates the session id from an MD5 hash seeded with a call to the built-in rand() function. The rand function is passed a maximum value based on the process id, the epoch time and the reference address of the object, but this information will have no effect on the overall quality of the seed of the message digest.

The rand function is seeded by 32-bits and is predictable. It is considere

title: A security vulnerability has been detected in npitre cramfs-tools up to 2.1. Affected is the...
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in npitre cramfs-tools up to 2.1. Affected is the function do_directory of the file cramfsck.c of the component Directory Handler. Such manipulation leads to path traversal. The attack can only be performed from a local environment. The exploit has been disclosed publicly and may be used. Upgrading to version 2.2 is able to address this issue. The name of the patch is 2fc492747115b24d8a07eddd27a2d45229cb273c. Upgrading the affected component is recommen

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in npitre cramfs-tools up to version 2.1 allows for path traversal attacks via the do_directory function in the cramfsck.c file. However, the attack is limited to local environments, reducing its overall impact and exploitability. If exploited, it could allow unauthorized access or modification of local files.
recommended_actions: Upgrade npitre cramfs-tools to version 2.2 or later to apply the patch (2fc492747115b24d8a07eddd27a2d45229cb273c).
Restrict local access to systems running vulnerable versions of cramfs-tools to trusted users only.
Conduct a review of systems using cramfs-tools to identify and mitigate potential risks associated with path traversal vulnerabilities.

title: A weakness has been identified in D-Link DNS-320 2.06B01. This impacts the function cgi_set_host...
description: A weakness has been identified in D-Link DNS-320 2.06B01. This impacts the function cgi_set_host/cgi_set_ntp/cgi_fan_control/cgi_merge_user of the file /cgi-bin/system_mgr.cgi. This manipulation causes os command injection. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to execute arbitrary OS commands, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or complete system compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply any available patches or firmware updates provided by D-Link for DNS-320 devices.
Restrict access to the device by implementing network segmentation and firewall rules to limit exposure to untrusted networks.
Monitor device logs for unusual activity and consider disabling the affected CGI functions if feasible.

title: A vulnerability was found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Impacted is the function...
description: A vulnerability was found in Open5GS up to 2.7.7. Impacted is the function smf_nsmf_handle_create_sm_context of the component SMF. Performing a manipulation results in denial of service. Remote exploitation of the attack is possible. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in Open5GS up to version 2.7.7 could allow remote attackers to perform a denial-of-service (DoS) attack by exploiting the smf_nsmf_handle_create_sm_context function in the SMF component. This could disrupt network services, particularly in environments relying on Open5GS for 5G core network operations.
recommended_actions: Upgrade to the latest version of Open5GS as soon as a patch is released by the project maintainers.
Implement network-level monitoring and intrusion detection systems to identify and mitigate potential exploitation attempts.
Restrict access to the SMF component by applying network segmentation and firewall rules to limit exposure to untrusted sources.

title: The Custom css-js-php WordPress plugin through 2.0.7 does not properly sanitize user input before...
description: The Custom css-js-php WordPress plugin through 2.0.7 does not properly sanitize user input before using it in a SQL query, and the result is passed to eval(), allowing unauthenticated users to execute arbitrary PHP code on the server.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary PHP code on the server, potentially leading to full server compromise, data exfiltration, defacement, or malware distribution.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or disable the Custom css-js-php WordPress plugin if a patched version is not available.
Implement a Web Application Firewall (WAF) to block malicious payloads targeting this vulnerability.
Conduct a security audit of the WordPress instance to identify signs of exploitation and ensure no unauthorized changes have been made.

title: CVE-2026-7372 - A stack overflow vulnerability exists in the WebCam Server Login functionality of GeoVision GV-VMS V
description:
A stack overflow vulnerability exists in the WebCam Server Login functionality of GeoVision GV-VMS V20 20.0.2. A specially crafted HTTP request can lead to an arbitrary code execution. An attacker can make an unauthenticated HTTP request to trigger this vulnerability.

#### Stack-overflow via unconstrained sscanf

The call to `sscanf` at [1] to split the `Buffer` variable into the `username` and `password` variables doesn't limit the size of the extracted content to match the destination buffers' sizes. In this case, if either the username or password decoded from the authorization string exceeds `40` characters (the size the stack variables  `username` and `password`) then a stack overflow will occur. 



The data is controlled by an attacker, but sronger constraints (e.g. no null bytes) may make exploitation harder. A successful attack could  lead to full code execution as SYSTEM on the machine running the service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7372.

title: CVE-2026-7371 - Multiple reflected cross-site scripting (xss) vulnerabilities exist in the Web Interface / ssi.cgi f
description: Multiple reflected cross-site scripting (xss) vulnerabilities exist in the Web Interface / ssi.cgi functionality of GeoVision LPC2011/LPC2211 1.10. A specially crafted malicious url can lead to an arbitrary javascript code execution. An attacker can provide a crafted URL to trigger this vulnerability. Reflected XXS via the error message for requesting non-existing page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of the victim's browser, potentially leading to session hijacking, data theft, or phishing attacks. This could compromise user data and system integrity.
recommended_actions: Apply the latest firmware update or patch provided by GeoVision to address the vulnerability.
Implement input validation and sanitization on all user-supplied data to prevent malicious scripts from being executed.
Educate users and administrators to avoid clicking on suspicious or unexpected URLs, especially those containing query parameters.

title: CVE-2026-7161 - An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVis
description:
An insufficient encryption vulnerability exists in the Device Authentication functionality of GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility 9.0.5. Listening to broadcast packets can lead to credentials leak. An attacker can listen to broadcast messages to trigger this vulnerability.


When interacting with various Geovision devices on the network, the utility may send privileged commands; in order to do so, the username and password of the device need to be provided. In some instances the command is broadcasted over UDP and the username/password are encrypted using a cryptographic protocol that appears to be derivated from Blowfish. However the symmetric key used for the encryption is also included in the packet, and thus the security of the username/password only relies on the "obscurity" of the encryption scheme. An attacker on the same LAN can listen to the broadcast traffic once an admin user interacts with the device, and decrypt the credentials using their own implementation of the algorithm. With this password the attacker would have full control over the device configuration, allowing them to change its ip address or even reset it to factory default.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to intercept broadcast packets containing device credentials, potentially leading to unauthorized access and control of GeoVision devices. This can result in data breaches, device manipulation, and network compromise.
recommended_actions: Immediately update GeoVision GV-IP Device Utility to the latest version that addresses CVE-2026-7161, if available.
Implement network segmentation to isolate GeoVision devices from broader network access and restrict broadcast traffic.
Enable encrypted communication protocols and disable plaintext broadcasts where possible to mitigate credential leaks.

title: CVE-2026-42369 - GV-VMS V20 is a Video Monitoring Software used to gather the feeds of many surveillance cameras and
description:
GV-VMS V20 is a Video Monitoring Software used to gather the feeds of many surveillance cameras and manage other security devices. It is a native application accessed locally, but it is also possible to enable remote access via the "WebCam Server" feature.  Once enabled, it is possible to access to the management and monitoring feature via a regular Web interface. This webersever is another native application, compiled without ASLR, which makes exploitation much easier and more likely. 



Most of the features require authentication before being reachable and leverage a standard login page to grant access. However the `gvapi` endpoint uses its own authentication mechanism via an `HTTP Authorization` header. It supports both `Basic` authentication and the `Digest` modes of authentication.  



#### Stack-overflow via unbound copy of base64 decoded string

The `b64decoder` string is sized dynamically, but it is then copied to the `Buffer` stack variable one character at the time at [0], and there's no bound-check. As such, if the decoded string is bigger than 256 characters (the size of the `Buffer` variable) then a stack overflow occurs. Because the data can be fully controlled by an attacker and lack of ASLR, this vulnerability can easily be exploited to gain full code execution as SYSTEM on the machine running the service.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with critical severity. CVE: CVE-2026-42369.

title: CVE-2026-6948 - Velociraptor versions prior to 0.76.4 contain a resource exhaustion vulnerability in the server's ag
description:
Velociraptor versions prior to 0.76.4 contain a resource exhaustion vulnerability in the server's agent control channel.



This allows a compromised or rogue Velociraptor client to crash the server via out-of-memory (OOM) by sending crafted messages through the normal client communication channel.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-6948.
